thesis: Between 2023 and 2025, large-language-model agents went from prompting curiosities to working systems that reason, plan, use tools, communicate with one another, and complete real-world tasks across software engineering, the web, and scientific research. This knowledge graph maps the classics of that three-year window, filtered to landmark works (≥ 500 citations) at top AI venues, organised around three coordinated lines of progress — single-agent reasoning + planning + tools + memory; multi-agent collaboration and emergent behaviour; and real-world agentic applications — plus a forward-looking Planning view distilling the field's next 18 months from each paper's own discussion and limitations.

paradigms.label: Reasoning, planning, tool use, memory
paradigms.summary: The single-agent core: how LLMs are made to reason step by step, plan over horizons longer than one prompt, call external tools, and remember across episodes. The foundational papers of agentic AI.

claims.claim: Explicit step-by-step reasoning prompts (Chain-of-Thought, Tree-of-Thought, Graph-of-Thought, Self-Consistency, Plan-and-Solve) materially raise LLM performance on multi-step problems and form the prompting backbone of every modern agent.
claims.search_terms: chain of thought tree of thoughts self consistency plan and solve reasoning prompt large language model

claims.claim: Letting an LLM emit structured tool calls (ReAct, Toolformer, function-calling APIs, HuggingGPT) turns it from a text generator into an agent that can search, compute, query APIs, and execute code — closing the gap between knowledge and action.
claims.search_terms: tool use function calling ReAct Toolformer language model agent execute API

claims.claim: Reflection / self-refinement loops (Reflexion, Self-Refine, Self-Discover) and persistent memory architectures (MemGPT, Memory-Augmented Transformers) let agents learn from their own mistakes within and across episodes — the bridge from stateless prompting to long-horizon autonomy.
claims.search_terms: reflexion self refine memory persistent MemGPT long horizon agent

paradigms.label: Multi-agent collaboration & emergence
paradigms.summary: When several agents share a goal, debate a problem, or specialise into roles, behaviours emerge that no single agent shows. The classics of 2023–2025 multi-agent systems: AutoGen, MetaGPT, CAMEL, Generative Agents, Voyager.

claims.claim: Role-playing frameworks (CAMEL, AutoGen, MetaGPT) and structured multi-agent debate / critique loops raise solution quality on tasks like coding, scientific reasoning, and decision-making by forcing models to surface and resolve disagreement.
claims.search_terms: multi-agent debate role play CAMEL AutoGen MetaGPT collaboration framework

claims.claim: Open-ended autonomous-agent loops (AutoGPT, BabyAGI, LangGraph-style state machines) plan, execute, and self-correct over many steps without per-step human supervision, demonstrating that LLM agents can drive multi-stage workflows.
claims.search_terms: autonomous agent AutoGPT BabyAGI self planning execution loop

claims.claim: Embedding LLM agents in persistent simulated environments (Generative Agents, Voyager, CivRealm) produces emergent social, exploratory, and skill-acquisition behaviour that no single prompt elicits — a substrate for studying agent collectives.
claims.search_terms: generative agents simulation emergent behaviour Voyager Minecraft persistent environment

paradigms.label: Real-world agentic applications
paradigms.summary: Where agents began to ship: software engineering (SWE-agent, Devin-style), web and computer use (WebArena, Computer Use), and scientific research (research co-pilots, biomedical agents). The classics that moved agents from demos to deployments.

claims.claim: Agentic systems for software engineering (SWE-agent, SWE-bench, AlphaCode 2, Devin-style code agents) solve real GitHub-issue benchmarks at increasing rates, demonstrating that LLM agents can plan, edit, run, and debug code over long horizons.
claims.search_terms: SWE-agent SWE-bench code generation autonomous software engineering agent debug

claims.claim: Web and GUI agents (WebArena, Mind2Web, computer-use models) and embodied LLM agents (PaLM-E, RT-2, VLA models) translate textual reasoning into clicks, keystrokes, and physical actions — opening up the action space agents can operate in.
claims.search_terms: web agent computer use WebArena Mind2Web embodied vision language action PaLM-E RT-2

claims.claim: Research co-pilot agents for biomedicine, chemistry, and materials (ChemCrow, Coscientist, Biomni, GeneAgent, agentic rare-disease diagnosis) demonstrate that agents can plan and execute multi-step scientific workflows — including hypothesis generation, literature synthesis, and wet-lab planning.
claims.search_terms: scientific research agent ChemCrow Coscientist Biomni biomedical agent hypothesis

anchors.5: strong, direct, explicit support for THIS specific claim

discovery.year_filter_note: Last three years only — 2023, 2024, 2025. Drop 2022 papers (CoT, Toolformer original, ReAct original) even though they are highly cited; the user explicitly asked for the most recent classics. If a 2022 paper has a 2023+ canonical version, prefer that.
venue_allowlist: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics
Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing
North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics
Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition
International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence

viewer.title: AI Agents — Classics of 2023–2025 — Knowledge Graph
viewer.subtitle: The most-cited LLM-agent papers from 2023, 2024, and 2025 (≥ 500 citations at top AI venues), organised by reasoning + tools + memory · multi-agent collaboration · real-world applications. Plus a Planning view of the field's next 18 months.
